title: Acceptance-testing guidance inlines one-use fmt.Sprintf helper arguments without flattening reused locals

provenance.originSummary: Derived from a sanitized real PR or review incident.
provenance.whyItMattered: Use this case to benchmark the fmt.Sprintf helper-composition rule so one-use helper locals are inlined without creating false positives on reused locals.
provenance.genericCondition: Sanitized fixture models an acceptance-test cleanup where single-use template/config locals are forwarded once into fmt.Sprintf, but one helper value is legitimately reused and should remain a local.

description: A sanitized acceptance-testing guidance scenario where helper functions return fmt.Sprintf-based Terraform config strings. The correct guidance should inline one-use helper calls like r.template(data) or r.basic(data) directly into fmt.Sprintf, while leaving reused locals alone.

scope.notes: Sanitized fixture models an acceptance-test cleanup where single-use template/config locals are forwarded once into fmt.Sprintf, but one helper value is legitimately reused and should remain a local.

mustCatch.description: The guidance should inline a one-use template local into fmt.Sprintf instead of keeping a temporary variable that is only forwarded once.
The guidance should inline a one-use config local such as r.basic(data) into fmt.Sprintf when the value is only forwarded once.

mustNotFlag.description: The guidance must not insist on inlining a helper local when the value is reused or materially improves readability.

notes: Use this case to benchmark the fmt.Sprintf helper-composition rule so one-use helper locals are inlined without creating false positives on reused locals.
